Parse + validate a remote payload. Unlike the plugin's

— a hostile or corrupt peer cannot inject a
structurally-invalid IR into the local scene. Throws on invalid input;
callers in observers must wrap this in try/catch (never throw out of a
Yjs observer).

to a stable JSON string with sorted object
keys. Two replicas observing the same logical IR must produce
byte-identical Y.Map values; otherwise last-writer-wins would flap on
key-order differences alone. Mirrors

Strip ASCII control characters (0x00–0x1f) and DEL (0x7f) from a display
name, then cap to

. Filters by code point to
avoid a control-character regex literal. Does NOT escape HTML — hosts
rendering the name into

must still escape; this only blunts
control-character injection.

when no provider is
mounted. This is the data seam for the deferred collab UI (I3-1): the

cursor/selection rendering ships, without changing the binding.

Error boundary for the canvas editor (W10). Without it, any render-time throw
— a malformed IR, a missing asset, or a bug in a host-supplied panel — unwinds
the whole React tree and the user loses the entire editor. Wrapping the panel
registry and the stage subtree contains a failure to that region: the rest of
the editor keeps working and the user gets a recover affordance instead of a
blank screen. Exported so hosts can wrap their own custom panels too.

Catches exceptions generated in descendant components. Unhandled exceptions will cause
the entire component tree to unmount.

Called immediately after updating occurs. Not called for the initial render.

content.text:
Single source of truth for the Canva-shell dock ids.

Kept dependency-free (no lucide/React) so both the Zustand UI store
(
) can import it without a cycle.

is derived
from the tuple so the union and the runtime list never drift.

when the host has not
configured one. Module-level constant so the reference is stable across
renders (no per-render allocation).
Built-in JSON exporter. Serializes the IR; round-trips back into the editor.
Built-in PNG exporter. Reads the live Konva stage directly — no extra deps.
Append a fresh blank page to the IR, commit

, and activate it.
Returns the new page id.
Brand-kit panel: the host's shared palette + fonts (I3-4), sourced from

when no brand kit is configured. Testids
(

Dynamic property toolbar — a floating, centered pill above the page (Canva
style). Surfaces the most-used quick props for the selected node
(fill · border · width · opacity), committing through the shared
pipeline. Renders nothing when the selection is empty.

The Canva-style editor shell: full-width Header · Aside (Panel Dock + Tab
Panel) · Canvas (dynamic Toolbar + Page with floating Element Controls) ·
full-width Footer. Composed over the headless

seam; UI state lives in a per-instance Zustand store provided
here. This is the editor's single top-level shell.

Deep-clone a CanvasPage with a brand-new page id and fresh ids on every
descendant node. Uses structured JSON cloning — safe because the IR is
pure data (per

is not mutated. The returned page has:
  - new
cloned with fresh ids (preserving transforms, bounds)

for the workspace header.
Pass it to

. PNG/JSON work with no
config; inject
Merge host overrides over the defaults (override wins per dock id).

(core's headless text-measurement contract) backed
by a real Canvas2D context — pass this to core's

) so an SVG export wraps rich text at
exactly the points the stage does. Both paths go through the same internal
layout function; only the glyph-width source differs from what the stage
renderer (
) uses, and it is the same source.
Build a fresh per-instance store. The persistence key is namespaced by
so concurrent stores never collide in

Delete a page. No-op when only one page remains (last-page guard — empty
IR would render the "no active page" fallback in
Trigger a browser download for an export artifact (client-only).

Deep-clone the currently active page (including all nodes with fresh ids),
insert it directly after the original, and activate the clone. Returns the
clone's id, or null if there is no active page in the current IR.

Floating per-selection controls pinned **above the selected element** (lock,
duplicate, delete, "⋯ more"). The toolbar is a DOM element so it doesn't
scale with the Konva canvas — but its position is computed from the rendered
canvas-pixel bounding box (via

The Canva-shell "Elements" panel — the new home for the drawing tools.
Renders each
entry as a button bound to

;
the active tool is highlighted. It is the drawing-tool surface for the

popover laid out after the
reference "Export image" panel (format cards · quality · resolution ·
metadata toggle · Export). Reads the live stage/IR from

and runs the matching exporter, then downloads.
PNG/JSON are built in; SVG/PDF (and overrides) arrive via

Serialize a live editor stage to a data URL with the editor-only chrome
layers hidden, so the exported preview shows only design content — not the
selection transformer, smart guides, or remote-presence overlays the user
happened to have on screen at export time.

Konva's

composes only visible layers, so we flip the chrome
layers invisible for the duration of the serialize and restore them in a

(the stage is normally about to unmount, but we never leave it in
a mutated visual state). Stages that don't expose

(e.g. unit-test
fakes) fall through to a plain
— there are no layers to hide.

Replace the current document's pages with a template's pages, as ONE undo
entry (canvas-m0-009 / FR-005).

Template pages are cloned with regenerated ids (a template can be loaded
twice, and its authored ids must never collide with the live document).
The batch creates the new pages first, then deletes the previous ones —
ordering that never violates the schema's one-page minimum. Undoing the
batch restores the prior pages and removes the template's.

Canva-style vertical multi-page view. Owns the scrollable canvas viewport on
the neutral-gray surface: the active page is the live editable Konva stage; the rest
are cached rasterized thumbnails (

, so the footer slider scales the
whole canvas uniformly.

Vertical icon rail (Aside, col 1). Each entry switches the active Tab Panel
via

; the active entry is highlighted and each
inactive icon can carry a configurable accent

and
return a data URL of its contents. The stage is built off-screen
(the container element is appended to the body but positioned far
outside the viewport) and torn down before the function resolves,
so callers do not need to manage lifecycle.

The render path reuses

so
non-active artboard previews match the live editor's output for
every node kind already wired into the renderer. Image nodes are
pre-decoded against

Rename a page. No-op when the page id is unknown or the new name matches
the existing name. Pass

) to remove
the explicit name and fall back to the default tab label.
Reorder a page in the IR. No-op when the page is already at

,
the page id is unknown, or

is out of range. Active page is
preserved (reordering does not change which page is editable).

Switch active page AND clear transient UI state — selection, draft (any
in-progress drag), text editor, and smart guides. Without the clears, a
stale

would point at node ids that don't
exist on the newly active page.

Tab Panel (Aside, col 2). Renders the registry descriptor for the active
dock id, with an optional search box, and dispatches by descriptor kind
(built-in / plugin / remote / search). Search resets when the dock changes
so each panel opens fresh.

— public, host-facing API (W1).

This root entry is the STABLE surface for integrating the canvas editor: the

shell, the context hooks, the
mountable panels + inspector fields, page/export actions, and the brand kit.

The editor's ADVANCED / EXTENSION internals (tool definitions, store
factories, stage primitives, snap engine, geometry helpers) are intentionally
NOT re-exported here — they carry no stability guarantee. Reach them via the
entry, or a specific deep subpath (e.g.

I3-3 a11y: a polite live region that announces the active tool whenever it
changes (

regardless of which surface — host toolbar, keyboard shortcut —
triggered the switch. Renders nothing visible.
Just the brand color swatches (empty array when none configured).

Just the brand font families (empty array when none configured). The
first entry, when present, is the brand default font.
(colors + fonts) from the Studio config, or

when none is configured. Always returns a
well-formed kit so callers never branch on

Read the stable store handles + callbacks WITHOUT subscribing to the live
per-commit state (W16). Use this in components that only touch stores/commit
and have no need for

, so they don't re-render on
every edit.

Falls back to the full

when the stable context is
absent — e.g. a partial test context that mounts only the merged provider.
The fallback

, unlike
other hooks): when the stable context IS present the merged context is never
read, so no per-commit subscription is created and the optimization holds.
chrome strings. Null-tolerant: when there is no
so the inline English fallback always renders.

bound to the current studio context.
Identical to the inspector's former local
Internal accessor for the active store. Throws if used outside a
so missing wiring fails loudly in development.

percentage readout, then the page indicator. Bound to

Full-width top header: Back · undo/redo · editable name · avatars · Share.
Undo/redo run the standard

wiring. The name is
click-to-edit only when
: an image node the host should offer contextual AI actions for.
: a drag-marquee region the host should fill with a generation.
I1-10 transient registry for in-flight AI jobs that back an
node on the canvas. The editor never runs jobs (the host does, via

lives with whoever runs the job (the host);
here we hold only an

closure registered when the job starts.

Purely ephemeral — never serialized into the IR (the placeholder node carries
the serializable
; this store carries the live cancel handle).
Correlation id shared with the placeholder node's
Cancel a pending job: fire its
once and flip status to cancelled.
Drop a settled job from the registry (host calls on completion/error).
Register a job the host just started (status begins
Maximum number of inverse commands kept in

. When exceeded, the
oldest entry is dropped.

(it can never be
longer than the most recent run of undos), so a single

call sharing the previous
call's merge key folds into the same undo entry. Default 400.
Optional deterministic clock plumbed into every applyCommand call.
Crop rectangle in the source image's natural-pixel space.

is the image
node being cropped (null when the editor is closed).

is the live,
in-progress crop rect updated on every handle drag — transient UI state, like

happens on confirm
(MVP-7: refs/transient state during interaction, one command on commit).

Tracks which IR node is currently being edited in-place (text overlay for
MVP-6 Task 7; could extend for future inline editors). Null when no editor
is open.
Apply many commands as ONE undo entry (a composite
array is a no-op and records nothing.
Apply a command, folding it into the previous undo entry when it shares

with the prior commit within the merge window (e.g. a drag or a
held arrow key). Intended for single-target edits whose inverse restores an
absolute value (move/rotate/resize/update) — not for structural commands.
Tracks which page in the IR is currently active in the editor. The IR's

array is the source of truth for the page list itself — this store
only owns the UI selection. Mutations to the IR's page list flow through
On-curve anchor vs. off-curve bezier control.

Tracks which path node is in on-stage point-editing mode (I3-2). Null when no
path editor is open. The

reads this to render the
anchor/control handles for that node.
One pen-tool anchor in world coordinates.

is the *outgoing* bezier
control handle (absolute world point); the incoming handle is its mirror
about

. When the handle equals the anchor the adjoining segments are
straight lines.

is optional so the
structurally-wider studio context (where it is optional) also fits.
Multi-click state for the in-progress pen path (I3-2). Unlike

this persists across pointerup (anchors accumulate click-by-click); it is
reset on commit, cancel, and tool change. The single

fires only
when the path is closed/finalized (MVP-7).
Set the outgoing handle of the most-recently-added anchor (drag-out).
Endpoints of the visible guide line (covers candidate + target rects).
Coordinate of the alignment line in world space.
Rect of the thing being snapped (e.g. a dragging node) in world coords.
When > 0 and no edge snap matches, snap the top-left to this grid size.
Other (unselected) node bounding rects to snap against.
Maximum world-space distance for an edge snap. Default 6.
Snap, smart-guide, and align/distribute geometry for

.

Framework-free (no React/Konva): operates purely on world-space rects so the
editor, headless consumers, and tests share one implementation. Lifted from
the editor's

modules; the edge-pair matching is unchanged, only
re-expressed without tuple indexing to satisfy
Raw screen-space coords as returned by
CSS cursor value applied to the stage container while active.

Per-event context handed to every tool handler. Stable across an interaction —
the same ctx that arrives in
so tools can stash interaction state on it via a closure ref.

Apply many commands as one undoable transaction (a single undo step).
Supplied by

; optional so
lightweight tool-test contexts may omit it — callers must fall back to
per-command

MVP-7: hand tool NEVER commits — pan is view state, lives in viewportStore.
The MVP-7 single-command assertion test's negative case (active tool = hand)
expects history.past.length === 0 after a full down→move*→up sequence.

MVP-7: single click → host's pickAsset() resolves → exactly one undo step
commits. pointermove/pointerup are not implemented (no drag interaction),
so the MVP-7 down→move*→up sequence yields ≤ 1 undoable action.

If pickAsset rejects (user cancels) or returns an empty string, no commit
fires. The async resolution happens out-of-band — selection is set once
the promise resolves, which can be after the pointer interaction is over.

Clicking INSIDE a frame targets that frame (the innermost one, per

) and places the image as its clipped child rather
than dropping a loose image on top of it. Filling an image well may need two
commands (the child + the frame's placeholder), which is why this goes
through
— one gesture stays one undo step.

Pen tool (I3-2). Click to place anchors; drag after a click to pull out a
symmetric bezier handle. Clicking within

of the first
anchor (with ≥2 anchors) closes the path and commits. Enter/Escape (handled by
the

) finalize/cancel. Per MVP-7 the only history commit is the
final
— clicks/drags mutate the transient pen store.

MVP-7 rule: commit fires only on pointerup. pointerdown/pointermove update
the transient draftStore — never historyStore.

MVP-7: single click commits exactly one node.create on pointerdown
(no drag, no pointermove side effects). Editor overlay opens on the same
event — see

. pointermove/pointerup are no-ops so the
MVP-7 single-command assertion holds for the full down→move*→up sequence.

grid only applies when no edge snap is available on that axis. Guides are
only emitted for edge snaps, never for grid snaps.

), so it never intercepts pointer events.
